_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 8-28 Evidentiary Blood Draw (Revised 5/30/2023, MDHHS Reviewed 2023). Base Michigan template — MCA-specific selections not configured.
protocol_reference: Michigan System Protocol Section 8-28: Evidentiary Blood Draw — MDHHS Approved 5/30/2023
purpose: MCA OPTIONAL. Govern EMS provider performance of evidentiary blood draws for law enforcement purposes (e.g., suspected OWI — Operating While Intoxicated). Evidentiary blood draws are performed at law enforcement request. PARAMEDIC USE ONLY. MCAs must authorize this procedure, define approved collection kits, and establish chain of custody requirements. Patient welfare is the primary obligation — medical care is not delayed for evidentiary purposes.

sections.title: Authorization
sections.reference: Michigan Protocol 8-28: Evidentiary Blood Draw — Authorization
points: MCA OPTIONAL — only authorized if MCA has approved evidentiary blood draw procedures
Law enforcement must request the blood draw — EMS does NOT initiate evidentiary draws without law enforcement request
A lawful warrant or consent must exist per law enforcement — EMS providers do not determine legal authority; that responsibility belongs to law enforcement
Medical care of the patient takes ABSOLUTE PRIORITY over any evidentiary blood draw — the draw is not performed if it would delay or compromise medical care

sections.title: Procedure
sections.reference: Michigan Protocol 8-28: Evidentiary Blood Draw — Procedure
points: Use only MCA-approved evidentiary blood collection kits provided by law enforcement

Perform draw using appropriate aseptic technique with non-alcohol-based skin antiseptic (betadine/povidone-iodine preferred — alcohol-based antiseptic may affect sample integrity for blood alcohol level)
Collect sample per kit instructions; label with patient name, date, time, and provider name
Document: time of draw, collection kit lot number, law enforcement officer name and badge number, and chain of custody transfer
Immediately transfer the collected sample to the requesting law enforcement officer — document transfer in PCR and on chain of custody form
Do NOT alter, delay, or improperly collect the sample — evidentiary integrity must be maintained

sections.title: Patient Rights
sections.reference: Michigan Protocol 8-28: Evidentiary Blood Draw — Patient Rights
points: Patient refusal: if the patient refuses the evidentiary blood draw, do NOT forcibly perform it — notify law enforcement of the refusal
Law enforcement determines how to proceed with a refusal — that is a law enforcement decision, not an EMS decision
Unconscious patient: law enforcement warrant authority governs; EMS performs the draw if medically safe to do so
Medical care is NOT withheld because an evidentiary draw is refused

mca_selections_required.description: MCA must authorize Paramedics to perform evidentiary blood draws for law enforcement
options: Authorized — Paramedic with MCA-approved training and approved collection kits

mca_selections_required.description: MCA must specify the approved evidentiary blood collection kit(s) for use in the jurisdiction
options: MCA-specified law enforcement-provided evidentiary kit only
Any Michigan State Police-approved evidentiary kit

deterioration_flags: Medical emergency present — do NOT perform evidentiary blood draw until patient is stabilized; medical care takes absolute priority
Patient refuses evidentiary blood draw — do NOT force; notify law enforcement; document refusal
scope_notes: Law enforcement request AND lawful warrant/consent required
Medical care ALWAYS takes priority over evidentiary draw
Non-alcohol-based antiseptic (betadine/povidone-iodine) — do NOT use alcohol-based prep
Use law enforcement-provided approved collection kit only
Chain of custody: label sample; document time/kit/officer; immediately transfer to law enforcement
Patient refusal: notify law enforcement; do NOT force draw
Unconscious patient: law enforcement warrant authority governs
